advisory_instructions.orchestrator_override: ACTIVE — Registry-naming discrepancy confirmed. The exact name 'MCU Factory Verification Review Advisory' is not in the registry. The registry contains 'Signoff Advisory' (id: 22820a79-9842-4615-8eb0-5e402095555f, purpose: 'Produce signoff verdict from verification evidence') which is the functional equivalent. You MUST use 'Signoff Advisory' as the functional match — the same resolution pattern used in Stage 1 where 'Spec Advisory' served as the functional match for 'MCU Factory Spec Advisory'. Call list_advisories to confirm both are present, then run 'Signoff Advisory' (id: 22820a79-9842-4615-8eb0-5e402095555f). Save raw output to docs/advisory/verification_review_advisory.md. Document the name discrepancy explicitly in the advisory file.
advisory_instructions.do_not_stop: Do NOT invoke the STOP rule. The orchestrator has confirmed this functional match is authorized. Proceed with all review steps.

acceptance_criteria: Checklist must be updated using verification evidence (21/21 PASS)
verification_dashboard.html must exist and be non-empty
Reviewer must return a verdict field and rework_target field
If verdict is PROCEED_TO_PNR, rework_target must be NONE
inputs.rework_notes: No failures to classify. All 21 scenarios passed. Reviewer should confirm PROCEED_TO_PNR.
known_limitations: APB write-then-read value equality not enforced via BFM (non-blocking, covered at IP level)
SRAM MEM-03/04 data-integrity KAT delegated to fw_boot scenario (PASS)
ADC (mixed-signal) out of scope for RTL verification
